overview: Two major feature series landed on June 22: Leon Hwang's v7 11-patch series introducing global percpu data adds a new BPF_ADDR_PERCPU instruction class with full libbpf and bpftool skeleton support, and Mahe Tardy's v8 7-patch series adds a bpf_icmp_send kfunc enabling BPF programs to send ICMP packets from tc and cgroup_skb hooks. Additional highlights include an RFC from Gyutae Bae proposing atomic compare-and-delete semantics for hash maps, RISC-V BPF exceptions support from Varun R Mallya, tracing_multi link info from Jiri Olsa, and several verifier correctness fixes from Yiyang Chen.

patches.title: bpf: Introduce global percpu data
patches.author: Leon Hwang
patches.summary: Introduces a new BPF instruction class BPF_ADDR_PERCPU enabling global per-CPU data storage accessible directly from BPF programs without a map lookup. The series (v7, 11 patches) adds the core kernel instruction support, disallows interpreter fallback for BPF_ADDR_PERCPU instructions (requiring JIT compilation), extends libbpf to probe and support the feature, and updates bpftool to generate skeletons for percpu globals. This provides a first-class, low-overhead mechanism for per-CPU counters and accumulators that BPF programs can access with a single instruction rather than a map lookup.

patches.title: bpf: add bpf_icmp_send kfunc
patches.author: Mahe Tardy
patches.summary: Adds a bpf_icmp_send kfunc that allows BPF programs to synthesize and send ICMP (v4 and v6) packets in response to network events. The v8 series refactors netfilter helper functions nf_reject_fill_skb_dst and nf_reject6_fill_skb_dst into core IPv4/IPv6 so they can be reused by the kfunc outside of netfilter. Tests cover tc program attachment and cgroup_skb hooks for both IPv4 and IPv6, and include a recursion guard test.

patches.title: bpf: implement compare-and-delete (BPF_F_COMPARE) for BPF_MAP_TYPE_HASH
patches.author: Gyutae Bae
patches.summary: RFC proposing a new BPF_F_COMPARE flag for BPF_MAP_DELETE_ELEM that enables atomic compare-and-delete semantics on hash maps. A new compare_value field is added to the map element UAPI; the delete only proceeds if the current map value matches compare_value. This solves a TOCTOU race that exists when userspace reads a value and then conditionally deletes it in two separate syscalls.

patches.title: bpf: Add tracing_multi link info support
patches.author: Jiri Olsa
patches.summary: Adds BPF link info reporting for tracing_multi links, exposing the list of attached trampoline targets and cookie values through the BPF_OBJ_GET_INFO_BY_FD syscall. bpftool is updated to parse and display the new tracing_multi link info. This fills a visibility gap where bpftool link show would not reveal which functions a multi-attach tracing program was attached to.

patches.title: riscv, bpf: Add support for BPF exceptions
patches.author: Varun R Mallya
patches.summary: Implements BPF exception support for the RISC-V architecture, bringing it to parity with x86_64 and arm64. The series implements arch_bpf_stack_walk() for RISC-V to enable unwinding through BPF JIT frames and adds the exception entry/exit trampoline code. The BPF CI denylist entry for RISC-V BPF exceptions is removed once the implementation is complete.

patches.title: bpf: Reject flexible-array allocation types
patches.author: Yiyang Chen
patches.summary: Fixes the BPF verifier to reject programs that attempt to allocate kernel objects with flexible-array member types using kfuncs. Allocating a flexible-array type via a kfunc would produce an object of indeterminate size, which the verifier cannot safely track. A selftest is added to confirm the rejection path.

patches.title: bpf, unix: Guard sk_msg-dependent code behind CONFIG_NET_SOCK_MSG
patches.author: Jakub Sitnicki
patches.summary: Guards Unix domain socket sk_msg path code behind the CONFIG_NET_SOCK_MSG Kconfig option to reduce code size on kernels that do not need BPF sockmap support for Unix sockets. This mirrors how TCP/UDP sk_msg paths are already conditionally compiled, and avoids pulling in sockmap infrastructure for configurations that do not enable it.
